2026-04-20T10:46:21.8089479Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8090095Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8090553Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8090926Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8091248Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8091491Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8091650Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8091831Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8092001Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8092236Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8092439Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8092732Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:46:21.8092994Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-20T10:50:28.4839462Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T10:47:30.640713233Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58378,\"title\":\"[AnalysisServices] Docs: Fix package name, add code snippets, and enrich Key Concepts in README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/analysisservices/Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit eff4c040a (PR #58373) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe Azure SDK Bot incremented the package version to `1.2.0-beta.1` following the stable `1.1.2` release of `Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis`. This is the first beta in the new minor cycle, making it a good moment to address accumulated documentation debt.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Wrong package name in install command**: The README `Getting Started` section shows `dotnet add package Azure.ResourceManager.AnalysisServices`, but the actual NuGet package ID is `Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis`\\n- **No inline code examples**: The `Examples` section links only to an external samples page with no inline code snippets, making it impossible to understand usage without leaving the README\\n- **Key Concepts section lacks substance**: Contains only a link to general Azure SDK docs; does not explain the package-specific types (`AnalysisServerResource`, `AnalysisServerCollection`, `AnalysisServerData`) or the resource hierarchy\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory**: There are no snippet-backed test files, so no code is eligible for injection via `Update-Snippets.ps1`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### Fix the package name in the install command\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/analysisservices/Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the wrong package name in the install block\\n\\nReplace:\\n```dotnetcli\\ndotnet add package Azure.ResourceManager.AnalysisServices\\n```\\nWith:\\n```dotnetcli\\ndotnet add package Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis\\n```\\n\\n#### Enrich the Key Concepts section\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/analysisservices/Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the thin Key Concepts section with substantive content\\n\\nReplace the existing Key Concepts section:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n\\n### AnalysisServerResource\\n\\nRepresents an Azure Analysis Services server resource. Use this to get, update, delete, suspend, or resume an individual server, and to manage firewall rules and gateway status.\\n\\n### AnalysisServerCollection\\n\\nThe collection of `AnalysisServerResource` objects scoped to a resource group. Use `GetAnalysisServers()` on a `ResourceGroupResource` to obtain the collection. Supports creating, listing, and checking existence of Analysis Services servers.\\n\\n### AnalysisServerData\\n\\nThe data (properties) of an Analysis Services server including SKU, location, administrator identities, backup storage URI, and current provisioning state.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Create a sample test file\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/analysisservices/Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\\n**Action:** Create\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_HelloWorld\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateAnalysisServer()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateAnalysisServer\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            // Get a resource group\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            // Get the Analysis Services server collection\\n            AnalysisServerCollection collection = resourceGroup.GetAnalysisServers();\\n\\n            // Define the server\\n            string serverName = \\u0026#34;myanalysisserver\\u0026#34;;\\n            var sku = new AnalysisResourceSku(\\u0026#34;S1\\u0026#34;) { Tier = AnalysisSkuTier.Standard };\\n            var serverData = new AnalysisServerData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;), sku);\\n\\n            // Create the server\\n            ArmOperation lro = await collection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                serverName,\\n                serverData);\\n            AnalysisServerResource server = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created server: {server.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string serverName = \\u0026#34;myanalysisserver\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n {server.Data.State}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListAnalysisServers()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListAnalysisServers\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n ({server.Data.State})\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task DeleteAnalysisServer()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DeleteAnalysisServer\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string serverName = \\u0026#34;myanalysisserver\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            AnalysisServerResource server = await resourceGroup\\n                .GetAnalysisServers()\\n                .GetAsync(serverName);\\n            await server.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine(\\u0026#34;Server deleted.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ({server.Data.State})\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task DeleteAnalysisServer()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DeleteAnalysisServer\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string serverName = \\u0026#34;myanalysisserver\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n .GetAsync(serverName);\\n            await server.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine(\\u0026#34;Server deleted.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n This is the first beta in the new minor cycle, making it a good moment to address accumulated documentation debt.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Wrong package name in install command**: The README `Getting Started` section shows `dotnet add package Azure.ResourceManager.AnalysisServices`, but the actual NuGet package ID is `Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis`\\n- **No inline code examples**: The `Examples` section links only to an external samples page with no inline code snippets, making it impossible to understand usage without leaving the README\\n- **Key Concepts section lacks substance**: Contains only a link to general Azure SDK docs; does not explain the package-specific types (`AnalysisServerResource`, `AnalysisServerCollection`, `AnalysisServerData`) or the resource hierarchy\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory**: There are no snippet-backed test files, so no code is eligible for injection via `Update-Snippets.ps1`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### Fix the package name in the install command\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/analysisservices/Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the wrong package name in the install block\\n\\nReplace:\\n```dotnetcli\\ndotnet add package Azure.ResourceManager.AnalysisServices\\n```\\nWith:\\n```dotnetcli\\ndotnet add package Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis\\n```\\n\\n#### Enrich the Key Concepts section\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/analysisservices/Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the thin Key Concepts section with substantive content\\n\\nReplace the existing Key Concepts section:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n\\n### AnalysisServerResource\\n\\nRepresents an Azure Analysis Services server resource. Use this to get, update, delete, suspend, or resume an individual server, and to manage firewall rules and gateway status.\\n\\n### AnalysisServerCollection\\n\\nThe collection of `AnalysisServerResource` objects scoped to a resource group. Use `GetAnalysisServers()` on a `ResourceGroupResource` to obtain the collection. Supports creating, listing, and checking existence of Analysis Services servers.\\n\\n### AnalysisServerData\\n\\nThe data (properties) of an Analysis Services server including SKU, location, administrator identities, backup storage URI, and current provisioning state.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Create a sample test file\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/analysisservices/Azure.ResourceManager.Analysis/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\\n**Action:** Create\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
